LAW AND MOTION 301, FEB-04-2026 RE: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT THE SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF ANNETTE RIBEIRO'S THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT (TAC) IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE RETALIATION CAUSES OF ACTION (FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION (VIOLATION OF LABOR CODE, SEC 1102.5), AND FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION (FEHA RETALIATION)), SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE HARASSMENT CAUSES OF ACTION (THIRD, NINTH, AND ELEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION), AND OVERRULED AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR ASSOCIATIONAL DISCRIMINATION. SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING. A PROPOSED ORDER HAS BEEN SUBMITTED AS INDICATED IN THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO THE OCT-17-2025 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT SFUSD'S DEMURRER TO ALL TWELVE CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF ANNETTE RIBEIRO IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN COURT DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1 (LABOR CODE RETALIATION), 3 (AGE HARASSMENT), 4 (FEHA RETALIATION), 5 (ASSOCIATIONAL DISCRIMINATION), 7 (NATIONAL ORIGIN HARASSMENT), 9 (RACE HARASSMENT), AND 11 (SEX HARASSMENT) AND IS OVERRULED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 2 (AGE DISCRIMINATION), 6 (NATIONAL ORIGIN DISCRIMINATION), 8 (RACE DISCRIMINATION), 10 (SEX DISCRIMINATION), AND 12 (FAILURE TO PREVENT DISCRIMINATION). (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/HEK)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-04-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's DEMURRER to 3RD Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant the San Francisco Unified School District's demurrer to plaintiff Annette Ribeiro's third amended complaint (TAC) is sustained with leave to amend as to the retaliation causes of action (first cause of action (violation of Labor Code, Sec. 1102.5), and fourth cause of action (FEHA retaliation)), sustained without leave to amend as to the harassment causes of action (third, ninth, and eleventh causes of action), and overruled as to the fifth cause of action for associational discrimination. see Word minutes for complete ruling.

A proposed order has been submitted as indicated in the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-25-2025 Continued To Dec-03-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-17-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant SFUSD's demurrer to all twelve causes of action in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Annette Ribeiro is sustained with ten court days leave to amend as to causes of action 1 (Labor Code retaliation), 3 (age harassment), 4 (FEHA retaliation), 5 (associational discrimination), 7 (national origin harassment), 9 (race harassment), and 11 (sex harassment) and is overruled as to causes of action 2 (age discrimination), 6 (national origin discrimination), 8 (race discrimination), 10 (sex discrimination), and 12 (failure to prevent discrimination). (See word minutes for complete ruling).

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/HEK)
